testcase.name: test_timeout

failure.message: aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorCertificateError: Cannot connect to host api.unpaywall.org:443 ssl:True [SSLCertVerificationError: (1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1006)')]

testcase.failure:
self = <aiohttp.connector.TCPConnector object at 0x107ed8950>, addr_infos = [] req = <aiohttp.client_reqrep.ClientRequest object at 0x107da6e90> timeout = ClientTimeout(total=300, connect=None, sock_read=None, sock_connect=30, ceil_threshold=5) client_error = <class 'aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorError'> args = (functools.partial(<class 'aiohttp.client_proto.ResponseHandler'>, loop=<_UnixSelectorEventLoop running=False closed=False debug=False>),) kwargs = {'server_hostname': 'api.unpaywall.org', 'ssl': <ssl.SSLContext object at 0x1079d1eb0>} sock = <socket.socket [closed] fd=-1, family=2, type=1, proto=6>
async def _wrap_create_connection( self, *args: Any, addr_infos: List[AddrInfoType], req: ClientRequest, timeout: "ClientTimeout", client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, **kwargs: Any, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: try: async with ceil_timeout( timeout.sock_connect, ceil_threshold=timeout.ceil_threshold ): sock = await aiohappyeyeballs.start_connection( addr_infos=addr_infos, local_addr_infos=self._local_addr_infos, happy_eyeballs_delay=self._happy_eyeballs_delay, interleave=self._interleave, loop=self._loop, socket_factory=self._socket_factory, ) # Add ssl_shutdown_timeout for Python 3.11+ when SSL is used if ( kwargs.get("ssl") and self._ssl_shutdown_timeout and sys.version_info >= (3, 11) ): kwargs["ssl_shutdown_timeout"] = self._ssl_shutdown_timeout > return await self._loop.create_connection(*args, **kwargs, sock=sock) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1313: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/base_events.py:1112: in create_connection transport, protocol = await self._create_connection_transport( /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/base_events.py:1145: in _create_connection_transport await waiter /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/sslproto.py:575: in _on_handshake_complete raise handshake_exc /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/sslproto.py:557: in _do_handshake self._sslobj.do_handshake() _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ssl.SSLObject object at 0x10ae25810>
def do_handshake(self): """Start the SSL/TLS handshake.""" > self._sslobj.do_handshake() E ssl.SSLCertVerificationError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1006)
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/ssl.py:979: SSLCertVerificationError
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
self = <tests.test_cache.TestUnpywallCache object at 0x10aa22b90> example_cache = <unpywall.cache.UnpywallCache object at 0x1085af450>
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_timeout(self, example_cache): timeout = 1 doi = '10.1016/j.jns.2020.116832' example_cache.timeout = timeout example_cache.delete(doi) assert doi not in example_cache.content > await example_cache.get(doi)
tests/test_cache.py:63: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ unpywall/cache.py:116: in get downloaded = await self.download(doi, errors) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ unpywall/cache.py:192: in download raise RequestException unpywall/cache.py:181: in download async with session.get(url) as r: .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:1510: in __aenter__ self._resp: _RetType = await self._coro ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:779: in _request resp = await handler(req) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:734: in _connect_and_send_request conn = await self._connector.connect( .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:672: in connect proto = await self._create_connection(req, traces, timeout) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1239: in _create_connection _, proto = await self._create_direct_connection(req, traces, timeout) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1611: in _create_direct_connection raise last_exc .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1580: in _create_direct_connection transp, proto = await self._wrap_create_connection( _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <aiohttp.connector.TCPConnector object at 0x107ed8950>, addr_infos = [] req = <aiohttp.client_reqrep.ClientRequest object at 0x107da6e90> timeout = ClientTimeout(total=300, connect=None, sock_read=None, sock_connect=30, ceil_threshold=5) client_error = <class 'aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorError'> args = (functools.partial(<class 'aiohttp.client_proto.ResponseHandler'>, loop=<_UnixSelectorEventLoop running=False closed=False debug=False>),) kwargs = {'server_hostname': 'api.unpaywall.org', 'ssl': <ssl.SSLContext object at 0x1079d1eb0>} sock = <socket.socket [closed] fd=-1, family=2, type=1, proto=6>
async def _wrap_create_connection( self, *args: Any, addr_infos: List[AddrInfoType], req: ClientRequest, timeout: "ClientTimeout", client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, **kwargs: Any, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: try: async with ceil_timeout( timeout.sock_connect, ceil_threshold=timeout.ceil_threshold ): sock = await aiohappyeyeballs.start_connection( addr_infos=addr_infos, local_addr_infos=self._local_addr_infos, happy_eyeballs_delay=self._happy_eyeballs_delay, interleave=self._interleave, loop=self._loop, socket_factory=self._socket_factory, ) # Add ssl_shutdown_timeout for Python 3.11+ when SSL is used if ( kwargs.get("ssl") and self._ssl_shutdown_timeout and sys.version_info >= (3, 11) ): kwargs["ssl_shutdown_timeout"] = self._ssl_shutdown_timeout return await self._loop.create_connection(*args, **kwargs, sock=sock) except cert_errors as exc: > raise ClientConnectorCertificateError(req.connection_key, exc) from exc E aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorCertificateError: Cannot connect to host api.unpaywall.org:443 ssl:True [SSLCertVerificationError: (1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1006)')]

testcase.name: test_main
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_cli.TestUnpywallCli object at 0x10aa52610>
@pytest.mark.asyncio @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_main(self):
with pytest.raises(SystemExit) as pytest_raise_system_exit: > assert await main(test_args=['-h']) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: __init__() should return None, not 'coroutine'
tests/test_cli.py:16: TypeError

testcase.name: test_view
test_download
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_cli.TestUnpywallCli object at 0x10aa53350> capfd = <_pytest.capture.CaptureFixture object at 0x10bcc8090>
@pytest.mark.asyncio @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_download(self, capfd):
bad_doi = 'bad_doi' > await main(test_args=['download', bad_doi]) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: __init__() should return None, not 'coroutine'
tests/test_cli.py:41: TypeError

testcase.name: test_link
test_get_df

error.message: failed on setup with "pytest.PytestRemovedIn9Warning: 'test_get_df' requested an async fixture 'Unpywall', with no plugin or hook that handled it. This is usually an error, as pytest does not natively support it. This will turn into an error in pytest 9. See: https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/deprecations.html#sync-test-depending-on-async-fixture"

testcase.error:
self = <Coroutine test_get_df>
def setup(self) -> None: runner_fixture_id = f"_{self._loop_scope}_scoped_runner" if runner_fixture_id not in self.fixturenames: self.fixturenames.append(runner_fixture_id) > return super().setup() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pytest_asyncio/plugin.py:458: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
fixturedef = <FixtureDef argname='Unpywall' scope='function' baseid='tests/test_unpywall.py::TestUnpywall'> request = <SubRequest 'Unpywall' for <Coroutine test_get_df>>
@pytest.hookimpl(wrapper=True) def pytest_fixture_setup(fixturedef: FixtureDef, request) -> object | None: asyncio_mode = _get_asyncio_mode(request.config) if not _is_asyncio_fixture_function(fixturedef.func): if asyncio_mode == Mode.STRICT: # Ignore async fixtures without explicit asyncio mark in strict mode # This applies to pytest_trio fixtures, for example > return (yield) ^^^^^ E pytest.PytestRemovedIn9Warning: 'test_get_df' requested an async fixture 'Unpywall', with no plugin or hook that handled it. This is usually an error, as pytest does not natively support it. This will turn into an error in pytest 9. E See: https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/deprecations.html#sync-test-depending-on-async-fixture
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pytest_asyncio/plugin.py:728: PytestRemovedIn9Warning
